Governing Law: Ala. Code § 35-4-20 et seq.; Ala. Code § 36-20-1 et seq.
Acknowledgment Form: Statutory form; notary certifies personal appearance and acknowledgment of execution
Venue Requirement: State and county where acknowledgment is taken
Signer Identification: Personal knowledge or satisfactory evidence (government-issued ID or credible witness)
E-Notarization: Permitted (including electronic notarization and remote online notarization)
Notarial Certificate Required Elements: Venue, date, name(s) of signer(s), acknowledgment statement, notary signature
Seal/Stamp Requirements: Official seal required; must include notary name, 'Notary Public,' state, and commission expiration date
Recording Requirements: Acknowledgment required for recording real property documents
Competency of Signer: Signer must appear willing and aware of the nature of the act; notary must refuse if the signer does not appear competent or is acting involuntarily

Governing Law: AS 44.50.010 et seq.; AS 09.63.010 et seq.
Acknowledgment Form: Statutory short form; notary certifies personal appearance and acknowledgment of execution
Venue Requirement: State and judicial district where acknowledgment is taken
Signer Identification: Personal knowledge or satisfactory evidence (government-issued photo ID or credible witness)
E-Notarization: Permitted (including electronic notarization and remote online notarization)
Notarial Certificate Required Elements: Venue, date, name(s) of signer(s), acknowledgment statement, notary signature
Seal/Stamp Requirements: Official seal required; must include notary name, 'Notary Public,' and 'State of Alaska'
Recording Requirements: Acknowledgment required for recording real property documents
Competency of Signer: Signer must appear willing and aware of the nature of the act; notary must refuse if the signer does not appear competent or is acting involuntarily

Governing Law: A.R.S. § 41-311 et seq.; A.R.S. § 33-501 et seq.
Acknowledgment Form: Statutory short form (A.R.S. § 33-503); notary certifies personal appearance and acknowledgment of execution
Venue Requirement: State and county where acknowledgment is taken
Signer Identification: Personal knowledge or satisfactory evidence (current government-issued ID with photo and signature or credible witnesses)
E-Notarization: Permitted (including electronic notarization and remote online notarization)
Notarial Certificate Required Elements: Venue, date, name(s) of signer(s), acknowledgment statement, notary signature
Seal/Stamp Requirements: Official seal required; must include notary name, 'Notary Public,' 'State of Arizona,' and commission expiration date
Recording Requirements: Acknowledgment required for recording real property documents
Competency of Signer: Signer must appear willing and aware of the nature of the act

Governing Law: Ark. Code § 21-14-101 et seq.; Ark. Code § 16-47-101 et seq.
Acknowledgment Form: Statutory form; notary certifies personal appearance and acknowledgment of execution
Venue Requirement: State and county where acknowledgment is taken
E-Notarization: Permitted (including electronic notarization and remote online notarization)
Notarial Certificate Required Elements: Venue, date, name(s) of signer(s), acknowledgment statement, notary signature
Seal/Stamp Requirements: Official seal required; must include notary name, 'Notary Public,' 'State of Arkansas,' and commission expiration date
Recording Requirements: Acknowledgment required for recording real property documents
Competency of Signer: Signer must appear willing and aware of the nature of the act

Governing Law: Cal. Gov. Code § 8200 et seq.; Cal. Civ. Code § 1181 et seq.
Acknowledgment Form: Mandatory statutory form (Cal. Civ. Code § 1189); All-Purpose Acknowledgment; must use exact statutory wording
Venue Requirement: State and county where acknowledgment is taken
Signer Identification: Satisfactory evidence required: valid government-issued identification or oath of one or two credible witnesses (Cal. Civ. Code § 1185); personal knowledge alone is NOT sufficient
Witness Requirements: Not required for acknowledgment itself; credible witnesses may be used for identification
Remote Online Notarization (RON): NOT authorized; California requires personal appearance before the notary
E-Notarization: Permitted (in-person electronic notarization); remote online notarization not permitted
Notarial Certificate Required Elements: Venue, date, name(s) of signer(s), exact statutory acknowledgment wording (Cal. Civ. Code § 1189), notary signature
Seal/Stamp Requirements: Official seal required; must include notary name, 'Notary Public,' 'State of California,' commission number, commission expiration date, county of principal place of business, and seal manufacturer's identification number
Recording Requirements: Acknowledgment required for recording; county recorder may reject non-conforming certificates; must use current statutory form
Competency of Signer: Signer must appear willing and aware of the nature of the act; notary must maintain a journal and obtain a thumbprint for deeds, quitclaim deeds, deeds of trust, and powers of attorney

Governing Law: La. R.S. 35:1 et seq.; La. C.C. art. 1833
Acknowledgment Form: Louisiana primarily uses 'authentic acts' rather than common-law acknowledgments; an authentic act is executed before a notary and two witnesses, though acknowledgments may still be used in limited contexts
Notary Commission Authority: Governor (administered by Secretary of State); Louisiana notaries are commissioned for life
Venue Requirement: State and parish where act is executed
Signer Identification: Personal knowledge or satisfactory identification; notary must identify the parties to the act
Witness Requirements: Two witnesses required for authentic acts; not required for all notarial acts
E-Notarization: Permitted (including electronic notarization and remote online notarization under La. R.S. 35:626 et seq.)
Notarial Certificate Required Elements: For authentic acts: parish, date, names of parties, signatures of parties, notary signature, and two witnesses' signatures
Seal/Stamp Requirements: Official seal required; requirements vary depending on whether the notary is an attorney or non-attorney notary
Commission Expiration on Certificate: Not applicable — Louisiana notary commissions do not expire
Recording Requirements: Authentic acts are self-proving and may be recorded in parish conveyance records when applicable
Competency of Signer: Signer must appear competent and capable of understanding the act; additional civil law capacity requirements may apply depending on the transaction

Notarial Certificate Required Elements: Venue, date, name(s) of signer(s), acknowledgment statement, notary signature
Seal/Stamp Requirements: Official seal required; must include notary name, 'Notary Public,' and 'State of Maine'
Recording Requirements: Acknowledgment required for recording real property documents
Competency of Signer: Signer must appear willing and aware of the nature of the act; notary must refuse if the signer does not appear competent or is acting involuntarily

Governing Law: N.Y. Exec. Law § 130 et seq.; N.Y. Real Prop. Law § 298 et seq.
Acknowledgment Form: Mandatory statutory forms (RPL § 309-a et seq.); form varies by signer capacity (individual, corporation, attorney-in-fact, etc.) and must substantially conform

Signer Identification: Personal knowledge or satisfactory evidence; signer must be known to the notary or identified to the notary
E-Notarization: Permitted (including electronic notarization and remote online notarization under N.Y. Exec. Law § 135-c et seq.)

Seal/Stamp Requirements: Official seal required; must include notary name, 'Notary Public,' and 'State of Oregon'
Recording Requirements: Acknowledgment required for recording real property documents with the County Clerk
Competency of Signer: Signer must appear willing and aware of the nature of the act; notary must refuse if the signer does not appear competent or is acting involuntarily

Governing Law: Tex. Gov. Code § 406.001 et seq.; Tex. Civ. Prac. & Rem. Code § 121
Acknowledgment Form: Statutory short form (Tex. Civ. Prac. & Rem. Code § 121.007); form varies by capacity and must substantially conform
Venue Requirement: State and county where acknowledgment is taken
Signer Identification: Personal knowledge or satisfactory evidence (government-issued photo identification or oath/affirmation of a credible witness)

Notarial Certificate Required Elements: Venue, date, name(s) of signer(s), acknowledgment statement, capacity statement where applicable, notary signature
Seal/Stamp Requirements: Official seal required; must include notary name, 'Notary Public, State of Texas,' and commission expiration date
Recording Requirements: Acknowledgment required for recording real property documents with the County Clerk; must comply with statutory form requirements
Competency of Signer: Signer must appear willing and aware of the nature of the act; notary must refuse if the signer does not appear competent or is acting involuntarily

Governing Law: Law No. 75 of 1987 (Notarial Law of Puerto Rico), as amended; 4 LPRA § 2001 et seq.
Acknowledgment Form: Civil law notarial system; acts are executed as public instruments (escrituras públicas) before a notary-attorney and recorded in the notary's protocol
Notary Commission Authority: Supreme Court of Puerto Rico (only licensed attorneys admitted to practice law may serve as notaries)
Venue Requirement: Municipality where the public instrument is executed
Signer Identification: Notary must identify all parties either through personal knowledge or reliable identification methods permitted under notarial law
Witness Requirements: Witnesses (testigos instrumentales) required only in specific cases (e.g., when mandated by law or when a party cannot sign); not universally required
Remote Online Notarization (RON): Not fully implemented in the same manner as U.S. states; limited digital and electronic notarization initiatives exist under evolving regulations
E-Notarization: Limited and evolving; Puerto Rico maintains a predominantly in-person civil law notarial system
Notarial Certificate Required Elements: Not applicable in common-law form; instead, the public instrument must include place (municipality), date, full identification of parties, statement of the act, notary attestation, notary signature, and inclusion in the notarial protocol
Seal/Stamp Requirements: Notarial seal required; must include the notary-attorney's name and professional identification (bar number)
Commission Expiration on Certificate: Not applicable; notarial authority depends on maintaining active attorney status and authorization by the Supreme Court
Recording Requirements: Public instruments affecting real property must be filed with the Property Registry (Registro de la Propiedad); validity is tied to proper execution and protocol inclusion
Competency of Signer: Parties must have legal capacity under the Puerto Rico Civil Code; notary must assess capacity and legality of the act
